title: Microsoft OneNote Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Microsoft OneNote** is the Microsoft 365 note-taking surface exposed through the Microsoft Graph v1.0 REST API at `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/onenote`. This connector ŌĆö `OneNoteConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "onenote"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) ŌĆö wraps the four-level OneNote object model:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Notebooks | `/notebooks` | List + read + create notebooks |
| Section groups | `/notebooks/{id}/sectionGroups`, `/sectionGroups` | List section groups |
| Sections | `/notebooks/{id}/sections`, `/sections` | List + read + create sections |
| Pages (metadata) | `/sections/{id}/pages`, `/pages` | List + read + create + update + delete pages |
| Pages (content) | `/pages/{id}/content` | Raw XHTML read + JSON-command PATCH |
| Copy / search | `/pages/{id}/copyToSection`, `/pages?$search=` | Cross-section copy + full-text search |

The connector normalises pages into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), honours Microsoft Graph throttling (`Retry-After`) plus refresh-on-401 token rotation, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ŌĆö all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::OneNoteHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Register an Azure AD app

1. Sign in to https://portal.azure.com ŌåÆ **Microsoft Entra ID ŌåÆ App registrations ŌåÆ New registration**.
2. Name the app (e.g. `Shielva OneNote`), choose **Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts** (multi-tenant), and set the redirect URI to your Shielva gateway callback (e.g. `https://gateway.shielva.local/oauth/callback`).
3. From the **Overview** tab, copy the **Application (client) ID** ŌĆö that's your `client_id`.
4. Go to **Certificates & secrets ŌåÆ New client secret**, set an expiry, and copy the **Value** (not the ID) ŌĆö that's your `client_secret`. Microsoft shows it only once.
5. Under **API permissions ŌåÆ Add a permission ŌåÆ Microsoft Graph ŌåÆ Delegated permissions**, grant:
   - `Notes.Read`
   - `Notes.ReadWrite`
   - `offline_access` (required to receive a refresh token)
   Click **Grant admin consent** if the tenant requires it.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ŌåÆ Manage Connectors ŌåÆ Microsoft OneNote**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (string) | Application (client) ID from step 3 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | Client secret VALUE from step 4 |
| `tenant_id` (string, optional) | `common` (multi-tenant) or your tenant GUID |
| `redirect_uri` (string) | Same URI you registered in Azure step 2 |
| `scopes` (string, optional) | `Notes.ReadWrite Notes.Read offline_access` (default) |
| `auth_url` / `token_url` (string, optional) | Auto-derived from `tenant_id` |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Defaults to `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/onenote` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Client-side soft cap, default 120 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `client_id` + `client_secret` and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)` ŌĆö the gateway then drives the OAuth code flow.

### 3. Complete OAuth

The gateway redirects the user to `auth_url` with the scopes, the user consents, Azure redirects back with `?code=ŌĆ”`, and the gateway invokes `authorize(auth_code, state)`. The connector POSTs to `token_url` and stores the resulting `TokenInfo` (access + refresh + scopes + expiry).

### 4. First health check

`health_check()` calls `GET /me/onenote/notebooks?$top=1`. On 2xx ŌåÆ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 ŌåÆ in-flight refresh; persistent 401 ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 ŌåÆ `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 ŌåÆ `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Microsoft OneNote uses the **OAuth2 authorization-code grant** against the Microsoft identity platform (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`). The connector handles the full lifecycle: authorization code exchange, access-token expiry tracking, and silent refresh-token rotation.

### Endpoint contract

```
Authorize:  https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize
Token:      https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token
Graph:      https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/onenote
```

`{tenant}` is the `tenant_id` install field ŌĆö `common` for multi-tenant, `organizations` for any work/school account, or a directory GUID/domain for single-tenant.

### Header contract

Every authenticated request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json     ŌåÉ omitted/overridden to application/xhtml+xml on create_page
```

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install(...)` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Persists config. Returns `(HEALTHY, PENDING)` so the gateway can drive the OAuth flow. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | Form-POSTs `grant_type=authorization_code` to `token_url`. Stores the resulting `TokenInfo`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /notebooks?$top=1`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Inherited from BaseConnector ŌĆö refreshes silently if `expires_at` passed. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | Form-POSTs `grant_type=refresh_token` to `token_url`. Rotates the refresh token if Microsoft returns a new one. |
| HTTP client `refresh-on-401` | Single auto-retry on the first 401: calls the connector's refresher callback, then replays the request. A second 401 surfaces as `OneNoteAuthError`. |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ŌĆö never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | Azure AD Application (client) ID |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | Azure AD secret value (NOT the ID) |
| `tenant_id` | string | no (default `common`) | `self.azure_tenant` | Azure tenant ŌĆö distinct from Shielva's `self.tenant_id` |
| `redirect_uri` | string | yes | `self.config['redirect_uri']` | Must match Azure app registration |
| `scopes` | string | no | `self.scopes` (defaults to `Notes.ReadWrite Notes.Read offline_access`) | Space-separated Graph scopes |
| `auth_url` | string | no | `self.auth_url` (auto-computed) | Override for sovereign clouds |
| `token_url` | string | no | `self.token_url` (auto-computed) | Override for sovereign clouds |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/onenote`) | Override for `/users/{id}/onenote` app-context calls |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no (default 120) | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
REQUIRED_SCOPES      = ["Notes.ReadWrite", "Notes.Read", "offline_access"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_GRAPH_BASE = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/onenote"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `OneNoteConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::OneNoteHTTPClient`; normalization lives in `helpers/normalizer.py`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`; persist via `save_config(...)`. Does NOT call Graph. Returns `(HEALTHY, PENDING)` if creds present; `(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` otherwise.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Exchanges the OAuth code for tokens at `token_url`. Persists via `set_token(...)`. Raises `OneNoteAuthError` on 401.

### `async on_token_refresh() -> TokenInfo`
Exchanges the stored refresh token for a new access token. Called automatically by `ensure_token()` when expiry has passed. Raises `RefreshError` if no refresh token is stored.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 notebook via `GET /notebooks?$top=1`. Classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Page stubs via `list_pages`, fetches each `/pages/{id}/content` XHTML, normalises to `NormalizedDocument`, calls `ingest_document(...)`. Incremental watermark = `lastModifiedDateTime` stored via `set_metadata('last_modified_at', ...)`; `full=True` resets it.

children.title: Notebooks
children.content:
### `async list_notebooks(*, top=25, skip=0, filter=None, orderby=None) -> Dict`
`GET /notebooks?$top=N&$skip=M&$filter=ŌĆ”&$orderby=ŌĆ”`.

### `async get_notebook(notebook_id) -> Dict`
`GET /notebooks/{notebookId}`.

### `async create_notebook(display_name) -> Dict`
`POST /notebooks` ŌĆö body: `{"displayName": "ŌĆ”"}`.

children.title: Sections + Section Groups
children.content:
### `async list_sections(*, notebook_id=None, top=25, skip=0) -> Dict`
When `notebook_id` is provided ŌåÆ `GET /notebooks/{id}/sections`; otherwise ŌåÆ `GET /sections` (all sections across all notebooks).

### `async get_section(section_id) -> Dict`
`GET /sections/{sectionId}`.

### `async create_section(notebook_id, display_name) -> Dict`
`POST /notebooks/{id}/sections` ŌĆö body: `{"displayName": "ŌĆ”"}`.

### `async list_section_groups(*, notebook_id=None) -> Dict`
When `notebook_id` is provided ŌåÆ `GET /notebooks/{id}/sectionGroups`; otherwise ŌåÆ `GET /sectionGroups`.

children.title: Pages (metadata + content)
children.content:
### `async list_pages(*, section_id=None, top=25, skip=0, filter=None, search=None) -> Dict` When `section_id` is provided ŌåÆ `GET /sections/{id}/pages`; otherwise ŌåÆ `GET /pages`. Supports `$filter` (e.g. `lastModifiedDateTime gt 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z`) and `$search`. ### `async get_page(page_id) -> Dict` `GET /pages/{id}` ŌĆö page metadata only (no body). ### `async get_page_content(page_id) -> str` `GET /pages/{id}/content` ŌĆö returns the **raw XHTML body** as a string, NOT a JSON dict. The HTTP client sets `expect_json=False` so the response.text is passed through verbatim. ### `async create_page(section_id, html_body, content_type='application/xhtml+xml') -> Dict` `POST /sections/{id}/pages` ŌĆö the body is **raw XHTML**, not JSON. The connector overrides `Content-Type` to `application/xhtml+xml`. Use `helpers.utils.build_simple_page_xhtml(title, body_html)` to assemble a minimal valid envelope. ### `async update_page(page_id, commands) -> Dict` `PATCH /pages/{id}/content` ŌĆö body is a **JSON array** of update commands, e.g. `[{"target":"body","action":"append","content":"

new

"}]`. Returns `{}` on 204. ### `async delete_page(page_id) -> Dict` `DELETE /pages/{id}` ŌĆö returns `{}` on 204. ### `async copy_page_to_section(page_id, target_section_id, group_id=None) -> Dict` `POST /pages/{id}/copyToSection` ŌĆö body: `{"id": "", "groupId": ""}`. Returns 202 + an async operation handle. ### `async search_pages(query, top=25) -> Dict` `GET /pages?$search=ŌĆ”&$top=N` ŌĆö full-text across all pages the user can access. ### `async get_page_normalized(page_id) -> NormalizedDocument` Convenience: composes `get_page` + `get_page_content` + `normalize_page(...)`. Returns a `NormalizedDocument` with id `f"{tenant_id}_{page_id}"`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
OneNoteError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ OneNoteAuthError                  # 401 ŌĆö token invalid/expired
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ OneNoteNotFound                   # 404
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ OneNoteRateLimitError             # 429 ŌĆö retry_after seconds
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ OneNoteNetworkError               # 5xx + transport failures
```

### Retry behaviour

Two layers:

1. **HTTP client (`client/http_client.py::request`) ŌĆö single in-flight recovery.**
   - **401** ŌåÆ call `token_refresher()` (a connector-supplied async callback) ŌåÆ retry once. Second 401 surfaces as `OneNoteAuthError`.
   - **429** ŌåÆ honour `Retry-After` (capped at 30s) ŌåÆ sleep ŌåÆ retry once. A second 429 surfaces as `OneNoteRateLimitError`.
2. **`helpers/utils.py::with_retry` ŌĆö caller-level exponential backoff.**
   - Retries on `OneNoteRateLimitError`, `OneNoteNetworkError`, and `httpx.RequestError`.
   - Delay = `max(server-supplied Retry-After, base_delay * 2 ** attempt + jitter)`, capped at 32s.
   - Default `max_retries=3`; methods that wrap mutations (create/update/delete) use the default; the no-op `health_check` uses `max_retries=2`.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   ŌåÉ logged as warning
5xx ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   ŌåÉ retried first, then surfaces
RefreshError ŌåÆ ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, EXPIRED)
```

Normalisation errors during `sync()` are caught per-document so one bad page doesn't abort the whole batch ŌĆö they increment `documents_failed` and the run finishes as `SyncStatus.PARTIAL`.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Microsoft shows the secret VALUE only once at generation ŌĆö rotate if lost. |
| `OneNoteAuthError: invalid_client` at authorize | Wrong client_secret OR the secret has expired in Azure | Generate a new secret under Azure ŌåÆ App registrations ŌåÆ Certificates & secrets, paste the new VALUE. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Graph ŌĆö scopes missing or admin consent not granted | In Azure ŌåÆ API permissions, verify `Notes.Read`, `Notes.ReadWrite`, `offline_access` are present and click **Grant admin consent**. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | Refresh token revoked (user signed out everywhere) OR refresh_token not granted (missing `offline_access` scope) | Re-run the OAuth flow. Verify `offline_access` is in the `scopes` field ŌĆö without it Microsoft never issues a refresh token. |
| `onenote.rate_limit ŌĆö retrying` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Graph per-app quota) | The client + with_retry back off automatically. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or reduce sync concurrency. |
| `OneNoteNotFound: 404 Not Found: get_notebook(...)` | Notebook ID belongs to a different user or has been deleted | Call `list_notebooks()` first to confirm the ID. Notebook IDs are user-scoped ŌĆö they don't survive a `tenant_id` switch. |
| `create_page` returns 400 `InvalidRequest` | Body isn't valid XHTML | The body MUST be XHTML with `<!DOCTYPE html><html><head><title>ŌĆ”</title></head><body>ŌĆ”</body></html>`. Use `helpers.utils.build_simple_page_xhtml(title, body_html)` to assemble it. |
| `update_page` returns 400 | Commands array malformed | Each command needs `target` (e.g. `body`, an element id, or `title`), `action` (`append`/`insert`/`replace`/`prepend`), and `content` (XHTML fragment). See https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/page-update. |
| `redirect_uri_mismatch` at authorize | The `redirect_uri` install field doesn't match Azure | Open Azure ŌåÆ App registrations ŌåÆ your app ŌåÆ Authentication and ensure the EXACT URI (including trailing slash) is registered. |
| `copy_page_to_section` returns 202 but no copy appears | OneNote copy is asynchronous | The 202 response includes an operation handle. Poll `/operations/{id}` (a future addition) or just re-list `list_pages(section_id=target)` after a few seconds. |
